idea-plugin.name: Mindmap - Call Graph Visualizer
idea-plugin.description:
Mindmap - Call Graph Visualizer

Visualize function call graphs as interactive mind maps inside your IDE. Place your cursor on any function and press Alt+G to view the call chain — callers, callees, and function depth — all in a Figma-style interactive canvas.

🌐 Language Support

  • Kotlin — Full support (K1 & K2), IntelliJ IDEA & Android Studio
  • Java — Full support, IntelliJ IDEA & Android Studio
  • Java ↔ Kotlin — Cross-language call graphs in mixed projects
  • Python — Coming soon

⚡ Getting Started

  1. Place your cursor inside any Kotlin function
  2. Press Alt+G (or right-click → Generate Mindmap)
  3. The Mindmap tool window opens with your function as the root node
  4. Callers appear on the left, callees on the right — color-coded by type
  5. Click any node to jump to its source code
  6. Double-click a node to trace deeper and merge its call graph into the view
  7. Use the Mini-Map (top-left) to navigate large graphs at a glance
  8. Open Settings (gear icon) to adjust trace depth (1-5 levels)

🎯 Key Features

  • Interactive Graph View - Hierarchical LR layout with callers left, root center, callees right
  • Tree View - Collapsible tree with type-specific icons and section headers
  • Live Mini-Map - VS Code-style overview with colored dots, viewport rectangle, click/drag-to-pan, and arrow key navigation
  • Hide / Unhide Nodes - Hover eye button to hide nodes with smart cascade (children auto-hide if disconnected); Hidden Nodes panel for easy unhide
  • Bidirectional Analysis - Outbound calls + inbound callers with configurable depth (1-5)
  • Abstract Function Tracing - Traces through abstract/interface functions to concrete implementations
  • Double-Click Trace - Merge a node's call graph into the current view
  • Cmd+Click Expand - Re-center the graph on any node with full history
  • Figma-style Zoom - 60fps scroll/pinch zoom with rAF batching, cursor-centered
  • Pan & Select Modes - Toggle with H/V keys; right-drag or middle-drag to pan anytime
  • Box Selection - Drag to select and move multiple nodes together
  • Navigation History - Back/forward (Alt+← / Alt+→) through explored functions
  • Search & Filter - Filter nodes by name across both views
  • Hover Info Cards - Signature, file location, depth, and LOC count
  • Settings Modal - Configure outbound/inbound trace depth with visual depth track
  • Dark Theme - Catppuccin Mocha theme with high-contrast panel headers

⌨️ Keyboard Shortcuts

  • Alt+G — Generate call graph
  • 1 / 2 — Switch Graph / Tree view
  • H / V — Pan tool / Select tool
  • F — Fit all nodes  |  C — Center on selected
  • + / - — Zoom in/out  |  / — Search
  • R — Restart / Clear graph
  • Arrow keys — Navigate nodes or pan minimap (on hover)
  • Space (hold) — Temporary pan in select mode
  • ? — Show all shortcuts

🖱️ Mouse Controls

  • Click node → jump to source  |  Double-click → trace deeper
  • Cmd/Ctrl+Click → expand (re-center graph)
  • Hover node → eye button to hide/unhide
  • Scroll / Pinch → zoom (Figma-style, 60fps)
  • Right-drag / Middle-drag → pan the viewport
  • Click / Drag minimap → navigate large graphs

Works with IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3+, Android Studio Ladybug+, and PyCharm 2024.3+.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.2

  • Live Mini-Map — VS Code-style minimap (top-left) with colored node dots, viewport rectangle, click-to-pan, and drag-to-pan navigation
  • Hide/Unhide Nodes — Hover any node to reveal an eye button; hidden nodes cascade-hide disconnected children and preserve their layout position across retraces
  • Hidden Nodes Panel — Bottom-left panel auto-appears when nodes are hidden; lists all hidden nodes with one-click unhide for easy access
  • Settings Modal — Gear button opens a redesigned settings panel with outbound/inbound depth controls, visual depth track indicators, and clear descriptions
  • Configurable Depth — Adjust outbound (1-5) and inbound (1-5) trace depth from the settings modal; takes effect on next trace
  • Abstract Function Tracing — Traces through abstract/interface functions to their concrete implementations (up to 3)
  • Figma-style Zoom — Smooth pinch-to-zoom and scroll-zoom with requestAnimationFrame batching for 60fps performance
  • Straight-Line Edges — Replaced cubic bezier curves with straight lines for maximum rendering performance
  • Crossing Minimization — Improved hierarchical layout with hubsize sorting, edge minimization, block shifting, and shake-towards-leaves
  • Performance First — Always-on high-performance rendering (disabled shadows/smoothing/animations)
  • Figma-style Interaction — Toggle between Pan (H) and Select (V) modes
  • Incremental Trace — Double-click a node to merge its call graph into the view
  • Restart & Clear — New button/shortcut (R) to wipe graph data and free memory

1.0.1

  • Fixed Android Studio tool window icon collision
  • Added Keyboard Shortcuts help modal (?)
  • Keyboard focus persists correctly in Tool Window after code navigation

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Interactive graph view with hierarchical layout
  • Tree view with SVG icons and continuous connectors
  • Box/marquee selection for multi-node movement
  • Right-click / middle-click drag for panning
  • Navigation history (back/forward)
  • Double-click trace with position preservation
  • Smart duplicate name detection with filename
  • Bidirectional analysis (outbound + inbound calls)
  • Large file optimization (300 node cap, batch limits)
  • Crash-proof error handling across all entry points
  • JCEF fallback panel for Android Studio
  • Catppuccin Mocha dark theme

action.description: Generate a call graph for the selected function
